title: [arcilator] Why use a Mux to reset a register, instead of creating a CompRegOp with the reset signal and the reset value?

features.name: register with synchronous reset signal
features.description: Code should include a clocked register that has an explicit synchronous reset signal and reset value, where the reset drives the next-value selection

features.name: register with non-zero preset initial value
features.description: Code should include a clocked register declared with a non-zero initial preset value that must be materialized at simulation start

features.name: mux replacing register reset port during lowering
features.description: Code should include a multiplexer selecting between a reset value and a next value based on a reset condition, as a substitute for a native register reset port
